summary: Audit-only slice. Scanned 82 app-target Swift files for SwiftUI initializers that take a title/label/key argument and cross-referenced against the 316 keys in Localizable.strings. Found 400 candidate visible-string call sites: 116 must-fix bypasses, 37 should-fix (suspect Button literals), 247 low/ok (already-localized literals, user content, intentional English, or debug-internal). Wrote docs/workflows/localization-visible-string-audit.md as the catalog and queued 10 narrow follow-up slices (one per surface + cross-cutting interpolation/Button-verify/accessibility). Did NOT change app resources, view code, or per-locale return files. Did NOT make any native-review claim.
missing_proof_levels: running-app-smoke for translated content per locale

contract_status_changes.before: No catalog existed for SwiftUI initializers that bypass Localizable.strings even when the key is present. Commit 2dfd2d0 fixed the user-reported gaps (Train Section/Label literals, Today Check-in, Write Capture button) but the broader pattern was uncatalogued.
contract_status_changes.after: docs/workflows/localization-visible-string-audit.md is the canonical catalog. 116 must-fix call sites identified across Today (73) / Home (54) / Write (31) / Train (19) / Career (13) / Root tabs (5) / Widgets (3). 9 classification categories. Tier mapping (must-fix / should-fix / low/ok). 10 narrow follow-up slices queued. localization-string-inventory.md and localization-dynamic-formatting.md cross-reference the audit. roadmap-status.md updates the Localization row to note the audit existence and the queued follow-ups.

residual_risks: The audit is a static heuristic. Some `bypass-label-literal` findings may localize correctly on iOS 17 even though the static pattern is suspect. Each per-surface fix slice should verify behavior on simulator under -AppleLanguages before mass-converting.

The classifier treats `Text(varString)` aggressively: it splits into ok-user-content (e.g., note.title), ok-localized-display-helper (e.g., stage.localizedDisplayName), and bypass-string-var-true (everything else). The heuristic uses substring matching; edge cases may be misclassified. Each fix slice must inspect upstream before deciding.

Button literal classification (`bypass-button-literal-suspect`) is a guess. Single-argument Button("...") literals empirically DO localize on iOS 17 in most projects. The follow-up slice `app-localization-button-literal-verify` is explicitly an audit-then-fix to avoid mass-converting things that already work.

The 17 `missing-key` findings are almost all interpolated copy ("Next: \(x)") that should NOT be added as keys verbatim — they need presentation formatters with positional substitution. The `app-localization-string-interpolation-formatters` slice owns that work.

The `Text("Focus lives in Continue. ...")` footer in TodayView line 279 was classified ok-literal-text because the literal + key exist. The user-reported observation that it doesn't translate is unexplained by the audit. If the gap reproduces, follow-up investigation is needed (simulator cache, deployment target, or a SwiftUI iOS-specific quirk).

Native review remains outstanding for every locale. The audit and its follow-ups can route copy through Localizable.strings but cannot fix translation quality. `app-localization-native-review-intake` stays blocked.
The audit does NOT cover assets / image names, color names, sound names, or system-symbol names. Those are out of scope of visible-string localization.
recommended_next_reason: Highest must-fix count (73 across Today/Digest/BuildInfo views) and most user-impacting per the prior manual/TestFlight gap reports.
